name: Weapon Properties
summary: What the tags on a weapon (Finesse, Light, Reach, Thrown, Versatile...) actually mean.

sections.heading: Reading a weapon
paragraphs: Every weapon lists a few properties that change how you attack with it. They appear on the weapon's page and in the Attacks table on each class sheet — hover any property name there to see its meaning.

sections.heading: The properties
list: Finesse — Use Strength or Dexterity (your choice) for your attack and damage rolls with it. (A class feature may further change which ability you use — e.g. the Joker uses Charisma.)
Light — Small and easy to handle. Holding a Light weapon in each hand lets you use a bonus action to make one extra attack with the second weapon (two-weapon fighting).
Heavy — Large and unwieldy; Small creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls made with it.
Reach — Adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, and for opportunity attacks.
Thrown — You can throw it to make a ranged attack, using the same ability modifier you would for a melee attack with it.
Two-handed — You need both hands to attack with it.
Versatile — You can use it one- or two-handed; used two-handed it deals the larger 'versatile' damage die shown on its page.
Ammunition — It needs ammunition to make a ranged attack; you draw a piece as part of the attack.
Loading — You can fire only one piece of ammunition when you attack with it, regardless of how many attacks you could normally make.
Special — It has unusual rules described in the weapon's own text.
